title: Phenomenon 26
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 26: Object Disappearances}} {{VoidedTheme}}__NOTOC__ =
Overview
=

'''Object disappearance''' is a phenomenon commonly found in unstable levels and levels with a high concentration of [[Object List|objects]]. It occurs when [[Object List|objects]] [[No-Clipping|noclip]] into each other, causing them to disappear without a trace.

=
Details
=

When large quantities of moveable objects are haphazardly clumped together, they may no-clip into each other, causing some objects to disappear. Usually, the object is found at another area in the level or even in other levels. However, sometimes the object can still be seen, but it cannot be interacted with anymore. Objects in this state are called '''"pre-disappeared objects"''' or '''"dematerializium"'''.
A pre-disappeared object carries the following properties:
*It can still be seen. *Light and other matter clip through it. *Its appearance glitches out and changes colours. *Its appearance cannot be affected by gravity and stays in the place where it pre-disappears. *The only way to destroy it is to be clipped through by a human body.
When a pre-disappeared object passes through a human body, it alters its shape and colour until it eventually fades out. Once the object has completely disappeared, retrieving it becomes impossible and what happens after the object has faded away is unclear.
Currently, researchers have proposed numerous theories to explain the occurrence of the phenomenon. But, only one of them is considered logical:
'''The Quantum Teleportation Theory'''
The majority of people believe this theory as it is the most logical regarding [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]]. Since quantum fluctuations are more common in unstable levels, objects in these levels may be affected and [[No-Clipping|no-clip]] into an unknown level, or possibly, [[The Broken]]. This explains why it is not possible to find the missing objects and the fact that the phenomenon typically occurs in unstable levels. The reason it also happens in levels with many resources is that there are more objects to clip into each other.

=
Additional Information
=

The most affected levels include but are not limited to [[Level 0]], [[Level 6]], [[Level 610]], [[The End]], and [[Level 399]]. The phenomenon only applies to [[Object List|objects from the Backrooms]], such as [[Object 1|almond water]] or [[Object 6|moth jelly]]. It does not affect objects from [[the Frontrooms]], including mobile phones and water, and the reason for this is unexplained.
The more common an object is, the easier it can be affected. For example, [[Object 1|almond water]] has the highest chance to vanish because it is one of the most prevalent objects in [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]]. The chance of a rare object disappearing is slim to none, while manmade objects are unaffectable.
